headline: Johor Property Glut Worsens With 14,800 Unsold Condos
context: NAPIC data showed Johor's residential property overhang reached 14,800 unsold completed units in 2025, the highest in Malaysia, driven by overbuilding of luxury condos in Iskandar Malaysia targeting foreign buyers who never came.

cards.big: 14,800 empty condos in Johor — RM11.2 billion in property sitting vacant while locals can't afford homes below RM300,000
cards.sub: Forest City alone has 28,000 completed units with only 15% occupancy rate after 8 years

cards.big: Johor overhang value at RM11.2 billion exceeds state's entire 2025 annual development budget of RM3.8 billion
cards.sub: Developer debt secured against unsold Johor inventory threatens RM8.4 billion in bank exposure

cards.big: 84% of unsold Johor units priced above RM500,000 — while median Johor household income supports only RM280,000 mortgage
cards.sub: Young Johoreans commute to Singapore because they earn more there and still can't afford Johor housing

cards.big: Johor approved 42,000 new condo units between 2015-2020 despite oversupply warnings from NAPIC as early as 2016
cards.sub: State planning approval process has no mechanism to reject developments based on market absorption capacity

cards.big: Building luxury condos for foreign buyers who never arrived while locals can't afford basic housing is urban planning malpractice
Should state governments be required to reject new high-end developments when existing overhang exceeds 24 months of absorption?
